new_findings.title: Decider daily-report-2026-05-04T09-24.json missing push_status field
new_findings.evidence: grep push_status monitor/decisions/daily-report-2026-05-04T09-24.json returns nothing. The 05-24 report from earlier today contained 'push_status':'403_rescue_pending', 'commit_sha', 'push_failure_log', 'recovery_path'. Today's 09-24 report omits all four. FUSE wins.json size 468,753 vs GitHub 468,153 (delta 600B consistent with the 5 self-applied patches) implies the push did NOT succeed and the universal-pusher rescue path was taken. The reporting field is missing despite the rescue being active.
new_findings.diagnosis: Mechanical/judgment ambiguity. Either (a) decider considered the rescue path now routine and stopped flagging it, OR (b) the field write was inadvertently dropped this run. Either way, the operator/tinker pipeline-health visibility is degraded — a missing push_status hides whether a rescue is in flight.
new_findings.recommendation: Low-priority prompt nudge: decider.md should always emit push_status (success | 403_rescue_pending | 403_unrescued) in every daily-report. The field is cheap and the visibility is high-value. Defer to next Mode 4 unless the operator wants it sooner.
new_findings.deferred_reason: Decider prompt edit; better as a small PROP than a sub-finding. Not pre-empting Mode 1 followup focus.

claude_md_audit.skip_reason: Mode 1 focus run. Audit is required every Mode 3 run; revisit at next clean Mode 3.

context_load.skip_reason: Mode 1 focus run. Per-agent token-load tracking belongs to Mode 3.

other_side_considered: It would be tempting to upgrade FINDING-2026-05-04-001 to medium severity because missing push_status during an active rescue is the exact kind of reporting gap that lets bugs hide. But: (a) the actual rescue path appears to be working — workspace-sync is healthy, and the 10:03Z slot will flush wins.json — so there is no functional outage; (b) we cannot tell from one data point whether decider deliberately changed the convention vs accidentally dropped the field, and a PROP based on one omission may overweight signal; (c) the operator already has PROP-016 open about the larger NEVER_PUSH coupling — adding a second proposal about adjacent reporting hygiene risks proposal-fatigue. Keeping it low and watching whether the next decider run (13:13Z) re-omits push_status is the proportionate response.
